raw_input:
[4.0/5] Questionable Instructions Based on other reviews I'm expecting that it will take a few months to see progress. I am taking a point off purely for the questionable instructions. Precise language would be appreciated (it mentions ripping off skin? it says that you cannot consume spinach? it says that it cannot be used on dark, light, or tattooed skin?) and the many times where it is translated poorly do not inspire confidence. I will update the review if I see progress over the next few months. [5.0/5] Much easier to use than I thought it was going to be. At first I wasn't interested in an IPL because I had tried epilators and figured this was going to hurt. Then I read a few things on it and women were saying that it didn't hurt at all, in fact they said it was like blasts of ice cold air. So I decided to give it a try. Right out of the box I was impressed with the quality of this IPL hair remover. It is a solid unit and feels great in your hand! I love the overlay of gold accents contrasted by the neutral white color. The device is very aesthetically pleasing and looks like it could compete easily with the much more expensive ones. I do wish it came with a nice carrying bag or storage bag. But, I really can't criticize it for that because it is so reasonably priced!
Once you understand how to use this device, it is very simple to use. Getting to that place is rather hard because the instructions are not as clear as they could be and the print is super small. I went online and read up on how to use this machine before it ever got here, and I'm really glad that I did because it really helped me. I found out that are things you need to know before you ever order this unit, so you may want to check out some information online beforehand.
I didn't feel like it was an ice blast, you can feel the heat from this device while using it, so you know if you're going to burn yourself. In my opinion you would have to purposely have to ignore the pain to burn yourself because you feel the heat intensify as you leave it in one spot. This one only comes with one size head so it is a little tricky to use around your mouth area. It also came with sunglasses which I did end up using because the flashes were rather bright. They also don't recommend using an electric razor before because it can cause a rash, so they say to shave in the shower before hand.
I am very satisfied with this device and plan on using it about 3 times a week for the first few weeks, dropping down to 2 times a week for the next couple of weeks, and then down to 1 time until I don't notice the hair coming back in. Overall, I really like it more than I thought I would. [5.0/5] Time needs to get results 👍⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ [[VIDEOID:3fd963e7f67b6de43613a806ccfcdcf6]] Easy to use, works great just need time to get a complete job. Recommend brand,See my unboxing video above. [1.0/5] Not flashing I lost my instructions and I can’t get it to flash. Can someone help me? Is there a customer service number to call? [5.0/5] Works pretty well, just takes time (months) to see real results Reviewed by spouse:

I have delayed doing this review to be able to use this multiple times to see how well it responds. I think it’s actually making a difference I have been waiting at least four weeks between each time I use it on my legs and the hair on my legs is definitely coming in less and less. If you have done hair removal procedures in the past, you know that it takes a lot of time and you must follow the hair growth process and this could take up to six months to a year to get the results that you want. I would say that patience prevails…they usually do work whether it’s a laser hair removal process or using one of these little devices at home, it just takes time. [4.0/5] seems to slow/reduce growth over time I've tried some other types of these laser hair removal devices and the main difference between this one and the others is that the front piece is cooled to make it cold enough to offset the heat from the laser treatment. You still feel heat from it though, but the cooling effect of the front plate mitigates it to a fair extent.

In terms of effectiveness, I found that this slowed down or thinned hair growth after about three weeks of use. It's important to follow the instructions though as you have to have hair down first. It won't work if there is visible hair growth. The area of effect is a narrow band so it's not ideal for legs. It mainly works well for facial hair or small areas. Also, since laser treatment only works during active hair growth, it's necessary to keep treating repeatedly so that you hit a hair growth cycle.

There is a disposable razor and a pair of wrap around sunglasses included to help you with the treatment. The sunglasses are pretty good, but I still closed my eyes and shielded the top of the laser treatment gun with my hand when using it as the flashes are fairly bright and visible even with eyes closed and sunglasses on. It does take some time to start to see results though so you have to keep at it. This is easy to use though, and it has a lot of flashes available before the light cartridge runs out. [5.0/5] Easy and Effective Hair removal treatment always costs a lot. I'm so happy that I now find a way to do it at home. The machine is smaller than expected. It feels comfortable holding it and the buttons are reachable with my short thumbs. It is very easy to use. Press the flash button and that's it. It has different levels of intensity to fit the hair of different body parts. It may take more sessions than going to a paid treatment but well, I don't have to make an appointment anymore! I can do it whenever I feel great. Hair eventually grows back because of hormone change and this little machine is the best for "maintenance" too. I like that there is also a pair of protection glasses. Everything needed, even a razor, is included in the box. Love it. [3.0/5] Nice quality I like how it looks, and the quality feels expensive but the book says I should be seeing results by now and i'm not [3.0/5] Nothing yet Using for 3 weeks, have not noticed any change to hair growth yet, though I think it says takes 12 weeks to start to see change. Unclear as the instructions are absolutely horrible and confusing. On technical devices like this, it would be great (necessary) if the instructions were actually translated and not gobbledy gook auto translate or whatever these are. Very confusing and irritating trying to follow them. Actually had to google how to use safely.

This is a lower power unit than the Amazon top seller brand name IPL hair remover. The price is 1/3 of that unit, but this is half the max power of that one so you will need more treatments over a longer period of time.

The automatic flash function was scary at first, but the unit will only flash on good skin contact and it increases the speed of treating areas significantly. Even with the ICE feature, the head can get quite hot when zapping quickly the longer it is used, so treat sensitive areas first.

I've used it every other day for 3 weeks and don't see any appreciable difference yet other than the time to prepare (shave) the areas and treatment time is a few minutes less each week.

So far garbage instructions and I wish the cord were both a little longer and more compliant. Will update as needed. [5.0/5] Feature packed and effective IPL device for hair removal My husband has been using this machine for about three weeks. In that time, he’s been using it on his cheeks, lower face, and neck. It’s already slowed down the random hairs that grow on his cheeks and along his neck. He’s starting to see places along his mustache that are not as densely filled.

This is the second IPL machine we’ve used; this outshines the brand name German one in several ways:
1. The cooling feature makes this thing way more comfortable.
2. There’s a counter for the numbers of flashes
3. The auto mode is much easier on the hands- no need to mash a button the whole time
4. You can manually adjust the intensity.

IPL has changed my life; I went from shaving daily to shaving a few stray hairs seasonally. This is a fantastic and effective IPL to use. [1.0/5] Didn't Work This thing looks like a Star Trek phaser and is just about as functional. Could be user error, but I never got this to perform its designed task. I used it for a couple of weeks with no noticeable results. It does come nicely packaged with a mostly readable instruction pamphlet. Given my results, I wouldn't recommend it. [5.0/5] Low price with cooling Review for: BESTEK IPL Hair Removal Permanent Painless laser Hair Remover Device for Women Man 999,999 Flashes light for Facial Legs, Arms, Armpits, Whole Body, Best At-Home Use (White)

This one has great stats and price on it:

Pros
=cooling on a sub $100 IPL which is great. Although some argue they do fine without it.
=1M flash. Last year they had these as the same price with only 500K flashes. We have no idea how many flashes are needed but if you're sharing with others, it should last a while.
=Razor is better quality than some of the cheap ones I've seen
=4 colors, not that it matters

Cons
=not a gun design which is easier to hold
=only 36w power
Make sure you
=use safety glasses
=shave prior to use [4.0/5] IPL hair removal tool adds active cooling (ICE). Relative low-power (36W) The market for IPL (Intense Pulsed Light) devices for hair removal has exploded in the past couple of years, and you'll find a myriad of options on Amazon.

I've tried a large number of these IPL devices at this point, and I can say that these IPL devices all work pretty similarly, with only a few differences separating them. If you are looking for hair removal, then the most significant differences mostly amount to ergonomics (shape of the device and positions of the buttons) and whether it integrates "ICE" functionality to actively cool the region to which you're applying the light.

This is a mid-priced unit that adds ICE Cooling functionality and works reasonably well.

Specifics of this IPL device:

** Only performs hair removal
** Includes ICE skin cooling functionality
** LCD screen shows the number of flashes left and power level (1-5), plus whether ICE auto modes are on/off
** Rated for 36W (low end of power for a home IPL)
** Oblong shape
** Rated for 999,999 flashes

Operation is pretty trivial:

1) Attach the power cord to the IPL device (the device is NOT battery operated--you must have it plugged in).
2) Turn on the device via the power button, which is a button in the middle of the top of the device.
3) Choose the power level to use (you have five levels, and you cycle through them using short pushes of the power button). The level will be indicated on the LCD screen.
4) Place the front end of the device against the skin you are trying to treat and push the flash button (big button on the top of the IPL). There is a ~1s refractory period after flashing before you can flash again.
5) Ice technology, which actively cools the metal region that comes in contact with your skin.

The light is extremely powerful and could damage your eyes with continued direct exposure. Therefore, you should never look directly into the light and a cheap pair of folding protective eyeglasses are included in the box. The light is triggered by sensors in the front of the device. Using very slow-motion photography, I was able to capture a ~1/100th split-second snapshot of the light (see photos). But the light duration is extremely short.

You run the tool over the area where you want to remove hair and make sure you expose all the hair follicles in that region. You will not see any immediate results. The light signals your hair to stop growing, but it requires multiple treatments over several weeks before you will be expected to see results. Eventually, you should find a substantial reduction in the hair in the treated areas.

In the box, you get the tool, a power cord, a pair of eye protection glasses, a cheap disposable razor, plus instructions.

A word about the "ice" functionality, because this listing provides almost no detail (nor do those for other IPL devices):

Ice functionality is another term for "Local epidermal cooling."

This is given various names in listings, one of the most common is "ice function" or "ice-cooling". This is local cooling of the epidermal region (surface skin) in the region where the IPL light pulse is being applied. The high-powered light can be irritating to some people, and cooling the skin in that region can reduce the immediate irritation. (It does not improve/decrease the efficacy of the treatment). This cooling effect is achieved via a Peltier junction semiconductor and a good thermoconducting material at the head of the IPL device (frequently sapphire or aluminum) that transmits the cold that the semiconductor generates to the skin. (A heatsink and fan inside the IPL device remove the heat that the Peltier junction also produces).

Epidermal cooling is not required for efficient use, but it may make it less unpleasant to use the device for some users.

This IPL device is lower-mid-priced relative to other IPL devices on Amazon, but it does add the "ICE" cooling functionality. The rated power is 36W, which is on the low end of the range of power you typically see for these home IPL devices (between 30W and 60W).

Summary:

Pros:
** Integrates epidermal cooling (ICE)
** LCD readout

Cons:
** Not the cheapest IPL device you can buy
** 36W rating is at the lower end for these types of devices [5.0/5] Very simple and fast [[VIDEOID:8522f9386c54241244ac40c04f958706]] I finally am going to work towards getting rid of my unwanted hair, I have thought about it for years but the cost to get it done was too expensive and time consuming for me.

This is simple to operate, fast and I don’t find it painful , you can set it to the settings that is best for you.

I started on setting 2 and have slowed progressed to 3 , I use it twice a week, on my 7th week I will do only once a week (all layers out in the instructions).

This is a process that will vary from person to person depending on the settings and you pigmentation.

It does now work for people who are very blonde /or fair skinned , or dark skin or tattooed.

I have noticed after a few weeks my hair getting thinner already.

Even after you get rid of you hair you will still need to work to keep it that way for a while, there is a time line in how often to use it and how many weeks in the instructions.

I believe this is definitely the way to go if you are not interested in going to get this done at the much higher expense.

Be sure to wear the included eye protection 🕶 [5.0/5] Looks a little different than listing image but still works the same I am loving the newer generation of IPL devices that are a fraction of the price of the original ones that came to 9-10 years ago. This is much lighter, smaller, and faster. I have not used this long enough to determine if it will completely stop hair growth, but it has so far slowed down hair regeneration by 3x - which accurately reflects their predicted timeline for hair regrowth. [5.0/5] Easy to use! Very nice laser hair removal device. Comes with razor and protective glasses. Easy instructions to follow. This device has 5 energy levels and two modes to choose from. This device has advanced cooling technology. The cooling feature really makes this practically painless. It's light weight and easy to operate. It's ergonomic design makes it comfortable to hold whether your doing your legs or an area on your face or even your bikini or underarm areas.
It's definitely a process that you have to be committed to keeping in order to see results. After about 8 weeks of using this 2 - 3 times a week it says that a couple times a month will be enough to keep the hair from coming back. I haven't gotten that far into my treatment yet, but I'm committed to it and I'm very happy with the quality of this device! [3.0/5] A Simple to Use Hair Removal Laser But Something Possibly For The Long Game This hair removal laser is easy to use, however this is something with results in the long game. Results will not be immediate and will require constant applications over a long period of time before real results MIGHT show. Will update if any noticeable results start to happen over time but as of this current period of 16 days there are still not much change in hair thickness or growth. Do note that I have been using the lowest setting thus far as instructions state to start low and work your way up to stronger settings, and may have to start doing so as my body hair is on the fuller side. [1.0/5] It doesn't work. Gave it a shot. Don't waste your money It didn't remove any hair at all.i was very excited to use it and I tried a like 4 times with no results

response:
Most reviewers are happy with this IPL hair removal device, though nearly all stress that results take time, often months of consistent use. Many noticed hair thinning or slowing after a few weeks, but full results require patience and repeated treatments to catch different hair growth cycles.

The device gets praise for its cooling feature, which makes treatment more comfortable and less painful than expected. Reviewers like the build quality, the gold and white design, the LCD screen showing flash count and power level, the auto flash mode, and the five intensity settings. It comes with a razor and protective glasses. Several people compared it favorably to more expensive brand name devices.

On the downside, the instructions are widely criticized as poorly translated, confusing, and hard to follow. Multiple reviewers recommend looking up how to use it online instead. The power at 36W is on the lower end for IPL devices, meaning more sessions may be needed. The single head size makes small areas like the upper lip tricky. The device must be plugged in and is not battery operated. A few reviewers got no results at all and gave it one star.

The device does not work on very light or blonde hair, very dark skin, or tattooed skin. Most satisfied users recommend shaving before each session, wearing the included eye protection, starting on a low setting, and sticking to a consistent schedule over several months before judging results.
